findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/ClassGroup/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:106` defines `twoRank [Module.Finite ...]`; `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:202-204` proves the inherited theorem with the same `[Module.Finite ...]` hypothesis, using `Mathlib/FieldTheory/Finiteness.lean:97` `Module.natCard_eq_pow_finrank [Module.Finite K V]`.
findings.fix: Move this theorem before `variable [Finite (ClassGroup R)]` or give it the explicit hypothesis `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient R)]`; keep only the instance deriving that hypothesis from `[Finite (ClassGroup R)]` under the finite-class-group section.
findings.issue: `ClassGroup.card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_two_pow_twoRank` is placed under `variable [Finite (ClassGroup R)]`, but the statement only needs `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient R)]`, matching `twoRank`.

summary: One class-group specialization keeps a finite-class-group hypothesis even though the local definition and inherited theorem are already stated at the finite-dimensional quotient level.
